{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2ef433797c7e4d09bb38a357","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Peer monitoring — stale claim release and crash cleanup.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Detects crashed or stuck workers by reading heartbeat files and comparing\n4\t//! them against the configured TTL. For crashed workers (stale heartbeat +\n5\t//! dead PID), releases the claimed bead, removes the heartbeat file, and\n6\t//! deregisters from the worker registry.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! This module is designed to be called by the Mend strand (Strand 2). No\n9\t//! other strand should perform peer monitoring.\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `health`, `registry`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n12\t\n13\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n14\tuse std::time::Duration;\n15\t\n16\tuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\n17\tuse chrono::Utc;\n18\t\n19\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n20\tuse crate::health::{HealthMonitor, StalePeer};\n21\tuse crate::registry::Registry;\n22\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n23\t\n24\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n25\t// PeerCheckResult\n26\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n27\t\n28\t/// Summary of a peer monitoring check cycle.\n29\t#[derive(Debug)]\n30\tpub struct PeerCheckResult {\n31\t    /// Number of crashed workers detected (dead PID).\n32\t    pub crashed_count: u32,\n33\t    /// Number of stuck workers detected (alive PID, stale heartbeat).\n34\t    pub stuck_count: u32,\n35\t    /// Number of beads released from crashed workers.\n36\t    pub beads_released: u32,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\timpl PeerCheckResult {\n40\t    /// Whether any cleanup work was performed.\n41\t    pub fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n42\t        self.beads_released > 0\n43\t    }\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n47\t// PeerMonitor\n48\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n49\t\n50\t/// Monitors peer workers for crashes and stale heartbeats.\n51\t///\n52\t/// Constructed with references to shared infrastructure (bead store, registry,\n53\t/// telemetry). Call `check_peers()` to run one monitoring cycle.\n54\tpub struct PeerMonitor<'a> {\n55\t    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n56\t    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n57\t    own_worker_id: String,\n58\t    store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n59\t    registry: &'a Registry,\n60\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\timpl<'a> PeerMonitor<'a> {\n64\t    /// Create a new peer monitor.\n65\t    ///\n66\t    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n67\t    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n68\t    /// - `own_worker_id`: this worker's ID (excluded from peer checks)\n69\t    /// - `store`: bead store for releasing orphaned beads\n70\t    /// - `registry`: worker registry for deregistering crashed workers\n71\t    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter for peer events\n72\t    pub fn new(\n73\t        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n74\t        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n75\t        own_worker_id: String,\n76\t        store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n77\t        registry: &'a Registry,\n78\t        telemetry: Telemetry,\n79\t    ) -> Self {\n80\t        PeerMonitor {\n81\t            heartbeat_dir,\n82\t            heartbeat_ttl,\n83\t            own_worker_id,\n84\t            store,\n85\t            registry,\n86\t            telemetry,\n87\t        }\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Run one peer monitoring cycle.\n91\t    ///\n92\t    /// Algorithm:\n93\t    /// 1. Read all heartbeat files\n94\t    /// 2. For each stale peer (excluding ourselves):\n95\t    ///    a. If PID is dead → CRASHED: release bead, remove heartbeat, deregister\n96\t    ///    b. If PID is alive → STUCK: emit warning only, do NOT release\n97\t    /// 3. Return summary of actions taken\n98\t    pub async fn check_peers(&self) -> Result<PeerCheckResult> {\n99\t        let heartbeats = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n100\t\n101\t        let mut result = PeerCheckResult {\n102\t            crashed_count: 0,\n103\t            stuck_count: 0,\n104\t            beads_released: 0,\n105\t        };\n106\t\n107\t        for hb in &heartbeats {\n108\t            // Skip our own heartbeat (match by qualified identity).\n109\t            if hb.qualified_id == self.own_worker_id {\n110\t                continue;\n111\t            }\n112\t\n113\t            // Only check stale heartbeats.\n114\t            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n115\t                continue;\n116\t            }\n117\t\n118\t            let pid_alive = HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n119\t            let stale_peer = StalePeer {\n120\t                worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n121\t                qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n122\t                pid: hb.pid,\n123\t                pid_alive,\n124\t                current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n125\t                last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n126\t                heartbeat_file: hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n127\t                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n128\t                }),\n129\t            };\n130\t\n131\t            if pid_alive {\n132\t                // STUCK: alive PID, stale heartbeat. Warn only — do NOT release.\n133\t                self.handle_stuck_peer(&stale_peer)?;\n134\t                result.stuck_count += 1;\n135\t            } else {\n136\t                // CRASHED: dead PID. Clean up.\n137\t                let released = self.handle_crashed_peer(&stale_peer).await?;\n138\t                result.crashed_count += 1;\n139\t                if released {\n140\t                    result.beads_released += 1;\n141\t                }\n142\t            }\n143\t        }\n144\t\n145\t        if result.crashed_count > 0 || result.stuck_count > 0 {\n146\t            tracing::info!(\n147\t                crashed = result.crashed_count,\n148\t                stuck = result.stuck_count,\n149\t                beads_released = result.beads_released,\n150\t                \"peer monitoring cycle complete\"\n151\t            );\n152\t        }\n153\t\n154\t        Ok(result)\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Handle a stuck peer: alive PID but stale heartbeat.\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// Safety: never release the bead, never kill the process. Alert only.\n160\t    fn handle_stuck_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<()> {\n161\t        let age_secs = Utc::now()\n162\t            .signed_duration_since(peer.last_heartbeat)\n163\t            .num_seconds()\n164\t            .max(0) as u64;\n165\t\n166\t        tracing::warn!(\n167\t            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n168\t            pid = peer.pid,\n169\t            age_secs,\n170\t            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n171\t            \"peer is stuck (alive PID, stale heartbeat) — not releasing\"\n172\t        );\n173\t\n174\t        // Emit peer.stale telemetry for each stuck peer.\n175\t        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n176\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckDetected {\n177\t                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n178\t                age_secs,\n179\t            })?;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        Ok(())\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Handle a crashed peer: dead PID, stale heartbeat.\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// 1. Release the claimed bead (if any)\n188\t    /// 2. Remove the heartbeat file\n189\t    /// 3. Deregister from the worker registry\n190\t    /// 4. Emit peer.crashed telemetry\n191\t    ///\n192\t    /// Returns `true` if a bead was released.\n193\t    async fn handle_crashed_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<bool> {\n194\t        tracing::info!(\n195\t            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n196\t            pid = peer.pid,\n197\t            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n198\t            \"peer crashed — cleaning up\"\n199\t        );\n200\t\n201\t        let mut bead_released = false;\n202\t\n203\t        // 1. Release the claimed bead (if any).\n204\t        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n205\t            match self.store.release(bead_id).await {\n206\t                Ok(()) => {\n207\t                    tracing::info!(\n208\t                        bead = %bead_id,\n209\t                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n210\t                        \"released orphaned bead from crashed worker\"\n211\t                    );\n212\t                    bead_released = true;\n213\t                }\n214\t                Err(e) => {\n215\t                    // Release failure is non-fatal — the bead may have already\n216\t                    // been released or closed by another path.\n217\t                    tracing::warn!(\n218\t                        bead = %bead_id,\n219\t                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n220\t                        error = %e,\n221\t                        \"failed to release bead from crashed worker (may already be released)\"\n222\t                    );\n223\t                }\n224\t            }\n225\t\n226\t            // Emit peer.crashed telemetry.\n227\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n228\t                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n229\t                peer_worker: peer\n230\t                    .qualified_id\n231\t                    .as_deref()\n232\t                    .unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id)\n233\t                    .to_string(),\n234\t            })?;\n235\t        }\n236\t\n237\t        // 2. Remove the heartbeat file.\n238\t        remove_heartbeat_file(&peer.heartbeat_file)?;\n239\t\n240\t        // 3. Deregister from the worker registry.\n241\t        let dereg_id = peer.qualified_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id);\n242\t        if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(dereg_id) {\n243\t            tracing::warn!(\n244\t                worker = %peer.worker_id,\n245\t                error = %e,\n246\t                \"failed to deregister crashed worker from registry\"\n247\t            );\n248\t        }\n249\t\n250\t        Ok(bead_released)\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n255\t// Helpers\n256\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n257\t\n258\t/// Remove a heartbeat file (best-effort).\n259\tfn remove_heartbeat_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n260\t    match std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n261\t        Ok(()) => {\n262\t            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"removed stale heartbeat file\");\n263\t            Ok(())\n264\t        }\n265\t        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {\n266\t            // Already gone — not an error.\n267\t            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file already removed\");\n268\t            Ok(())\n269\t        }\n270\t        Err(e) => {\n271\t            Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to remove heartbeat file: {}\", path.display()))\n272\t        }\n273\t    }\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n277\t// Tests\n278\t// assert!(result.is_ok());\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    #[test]\n709\t    fn remove_heartbeat_file_removes_existing() {\n710\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n711\t        let path =      stuck_count: 1, // stuck doesn't count as \"work\"\n724\t            beads_released: 0,\n725\t        };\n726\t        assert!(!no_work.did_work());\n727\t\n728\t        let did_work = }\n735\t}\n736\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b2831981-46fc-4459-b8ef-f56f26eb02b6","uuid":"a2227dcc-eaa9-468a-8c09-22e2637cb268","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:35:19.874Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/peer/mod.rs","content":"//! Peer monitoring — stale claim release and crash cleanup.\n//!\n//! Detects crashed or stuck workers by reading heartbeat files and comparing\n//! them against the configured TTL. For crashed workers (stale heartbeat +\n//! dead PID), releases the claimed bead, removes the heartbeat file, and\n//! deregisters from the worker registry.\n//!\n//! This module is designed to be called by the Mend strand (Strand 2). No\n//! other strand should perform peer monitoring.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `health`, `registry`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::health::{HealthMonitor, StalePeer};\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PeerCheckResult\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Summary of a peer monitoring check cycle.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct PeerCheckResult {\n    /// Number of crashed workers detected (dead PID).\n    pub crashed_count: u32,\n    /// Number of stuck workers detected (alive PID, stale heartbeat).\n    pub stuck_count: u32,\n    /// Number of beads released from crashed workers.\n    pub beads_released: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PeerCheckResult {\n    /// Whether any cleanup work was performed.\n    pub fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        self.beads_released > 0\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PeerMonitor\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Monitors peer workers for crashes and stale heartbeats.\n///\n/// Constructed with references to shared infrastructure (bead store, registry,\n/// telemetry). Call `check_peers()` to run one monitoring cycle.\npub struct PeerMonitor<'a> {\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    own_worker_id: String,\n    store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &'a Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl<'a> PeerMonitor<'a> {\n    /// Create a new peer monitor.\n    ///\n    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n    /// - `own_worker_id`: this worker's ID (excluded from peer checks)\n    /// - `store`: bead store for releasing orphaned beads\n    /// - `registry`: worker registry for deregistering crashed workers\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter for peer events\n    pub fn new(\n        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n        own_worker_id: String,\n        store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n        registry: &'a Registry,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        PeerMonitor {\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            own_worker_id,\n            store,\n            registry,\n            telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run one peer monitoring cycle.\n    ///\n    /// Algorithm:\n    /// 1. Read all heartbeat files\n    /// 2. For each stale peer (excluding ourselves):\n    ///    a. If PID is dead → CRASHED: release bead, remove heartbeat, deregister\n    ///    b. If PID is alive → STUCK: emit warning only, do NOT release\n    /// 3. Return summary of actions taken\n    pub async fn check_peers(&self) -> Result<PeerCheckResult> {\n        let heartbeats = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n\n        let mut result = PeerCheckResult {\n            crashed_count: 0,\n            stuck_count: 0,\n            beads_released: 0,\n        };\n\n        for hb in &heartbeats {\n            // Skip our own heartbeat (match by qualified identity).\n            if hb.qualified_id == self.own_worker_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Only check stale heartbeats.\n            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let pid_alive = HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n            let stale_peer = StalePeer {\n                worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n                qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n                pid: hb.pid,\n                pid_alive,\n                current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n                last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n                heartbeat_file: hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n                }),\n            };\n\n            if pid_alive {\n                // STUCK: alive PID, stale heartbeat. Warn only — do NOT release.\n                self.handle_stuck_peer(&stale_peer)?;\n                result.stuck_count += 1;\n            } else {\n                // CRASHED: dead PID. Clean up.\n                let released = self.handle_crashed_peer(&stale_peer).await?;\n                result.crashed_count += 1;\n                if released {\n                    result.beads_released += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if result.crashed_count > 0 || result.stuck_count > 0 {\n            tracing::info!(\n                crashed = result.crashed_count,\n                stuck = result.stuck_count,\n                beads_released = result.beads_released,\n                \"peer monitoring cycle complete\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a stuck peer: alive PID but stale heartbeat.\n    ///\n    /// Safety: never release the bead, never kill the process. Alert only.\n    fn handle_stuck_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<()> {\n        let age_secs = Utc::now()\n            .signed_duration_since(peer.last_heartbeat)\n            .num_seconds()\n            .max(0) as u64;\n\n        tracing::warn!(\n            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n            pid = peer.pid,\n            age_secs,\n            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n            \"peer is stuck (alive PID, stale heartbeat) — not releasing\"\n        );\n\n        // Emit peer.stale telemetry for each stuck peer.\n        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckDetected {\n                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                age_secs,\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a crashed peer: dead PID, stale heartbeat.\n    ///\n    /// 1. Release the claimed bead (if any)\n    /// 2. Remove the heartbeat file\n    /// 3. Deregister from the worker registry\n    /// 4. Emit peer.crashed telemetry\n    ///\n    /// Returns `true` if a bead was released.\n    async fn handle_crashed_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<bool> {\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n            pid = peer.pid,\n            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n            \"peer crashed — cleaning up\"\n        );\n\n        let mut bead_released = false;\n\n        // 1. Release the claimed bead (if any).\n        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n            match self.store.release(bead_id).await {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead = %bead_id,\n                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                        \"released orphaned bead from crashed worker\"\n                    );\n                    bead_released = true;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Release failure is non-fatal — the bead may have already\n                    // been released or closed by another path.\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead = %bead_id,\n                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to release bead from crashed worker (may already be released)\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Emit peer.crashed telemetry.\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                peer_worker: peer\n                    .qualified_id\n                    .as_deref()\n                    .unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id)\n                    .to_string(),\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // 2. Remove the heartbeat file.\n        remove_heartbeat_file(&peer.heartbeat_file)?;\n\n        // 3. Deregister from the worker registry.\n        let dereg_id = peer.qualified_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id);\n        if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(dereg_id) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to deregister crashed worker from registry\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(bead_released)\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Helpers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Remove a heartbeat file (best-effort).\nfn remove_heartbeat_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n    match std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n        Ok(()) => {\n            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"removed stale heartbeat file\");\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {\n            // Already gone — not an error.\n            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file already removed\");\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to remove heartbeat file: {}\", path.display()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::{Filters, RepairReport};\n    use crate::health::HeartbeatData;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, ClaimResult, WorkerState};\n    use async_trait::async_trait;\n    use chrono::Utc;\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n\n    // ── Mock bead store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Track which beads were released.\n    struct MockBeadStore {\n        release_count: Arc<AtomicU32>,\n    }\n\n    impl MockBeadStore {\n        fn new() -> (Self, Arc<AtomicU32>) {\n            let count = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n            (\n                MockBeadStore {\n                    release_count: count.clone(),\n                },\n                count,\n            )\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.release_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"mock-bead\"))\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_dependency(\n            &self,\n            _blocked_id: &BeadId,\n            _blocker_id: &BeadId,\n        ) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    fn write_heartbeat(dir: &Path, data: &HeartbeatData) {\n        let name = if data.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n            &data.worker_id\n        } else {\n            &data.qualified_id\n        };\n        let path = dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", name));\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(data).unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(path, json).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    fn make_heartbeat(\n        worker_id: &str,\n        pid: u32,\n        bead_id: Option<&str>,\n        stale: bool,\n    ) -> HeartbeatData {\n        let last_heartbeat = if stale {\n            // 10 minutes ago — well past any reasonable TTL\n            Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(600)\n        } else {\n            Utc::now()\n        };\n\n        HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n            qualified_id: format!(\"claude-{}\", worker_id),\n            pid,\n            state: WorkerState::Executing,\n            current_bead: bead_id.map(BeadId::from),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            last_heartbeat,\n            started_at: Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(3600),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            session: worker_id.to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn healthy_peers_are_ignored() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a fresh (non-stale) heartbeat for another worker.\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"other-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-abc\"), false),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 0);\n        assert!(!result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn crashed_peer_bead_released() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a stale heartbeat with a dead PID (99999999).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"dead-worker\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-orphan\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 1);\n        assert!(result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n\n        // Heartbeat file should be removed.\n        let hb_path = hb_dir.join(\"claude-dead-worker.json\");\n        assert!(!hb_path.exists(), \"heartbeat file should be removed\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn stuck_peer_not_released() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a stale heartbeat with our own PID (alive).\n        // Use a different worker_id so it's not skipped as \"self\".\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"stuck-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-busy\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 0);\n        assert!(!result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n\n        // Heartbeat file should NOT be removed for stuck workers.\n        let hb_path = hb_dir.join(\"claude-stuck-worker.json\");\n        assert!(\n            hb_path.exists(),\n            \"heartbeat file should remain for stuck worker\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn own_heartbeat_skipped() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a stale heartbeat for ourselves (should be skipped).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"my-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-mine\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn crashed_peer_no_bead_still_cleaned() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Crashed worker with no active bead.\n        write_heartbeat(hb_dir, &make_heartbeat(\"dead-idle\", 99_999_999, None, true));\n\n        // Register the worker so we can verify deregistration.\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: \"claude-dead-idle\".to_string(),\n                pid: 99_999_999,\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n                agent: \"test\".to_string(),\n                model: None,\n                provider: None,\n                started_at: Utc::now(),\n                beads_processed: 0,\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 0); // no bead to release\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n\n        // Heartbeat file should be removed.\n        assert!(!hb_dir.join(\"claude-dead-idle.json\").exists());\n\n        // Worker should be deregistered.\n        let workers = registry.list().unwrap();\n        assert!(workers.is_empty(), \"crashed worker should be deregistered\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn multiple_peers_mixed_states() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Healthy peer (fresh heartbeat).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"healthy\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-1\"), false),\n        );\n        // Stuck peer (stale heartbeat, alive PID).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"stuck\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-2\"), true),\n        );\n        // Crashed peer (stale heartbeat, dead PID).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"crashed\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-3\"), true),\n        );\n        // Our own heartbeat (should be skipped even if stale).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"my-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-4\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"claude-my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 1);\n        assert!(result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n\n        // Only the crashed peer's heartbeat should be removed.\n        assert!(hb_dir.join(\"claude-healthy.json\").exists());\n        assert!(hb_dir.join(\"claude-stuck.json\").exists());\n        assert!(!hb_dir.join(\"claude-crashed.json\").exists());\n        assert!(hb_dir.join(\"claude-my-worker.json\").exists());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn empty_heartbeat_dir_returns_zero() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        let (store, _) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n #[tokio::test]\n    async fn nonexistent_heartbeat_dir_returns_zero() {\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let (store, _) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn remove_heartbeat_file_nonexistent_is_ok() {\n        let result = remove_heartbeat_file(Path::new(\"/nonexistent/file.json\"));\n        assert!(result.is_ok());\n Structured telemetry — JSONL event stream, never on stdout/stderr.\n//!\n//! Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits a typed\n//! event. The emitter is non-blocking: events are queued and written by a\n//! background task. A broken sink never blocks or panics the worker.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//! ```text\n//! worker → emit() → mpsc::Sender → [background task] → TelemetryBus → Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\nuse crate::config::{ColorMode, HookConfig, StdoutFormat, StdoutSinkConfig, TelemetryConfig};\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerId, WorkerState};\n\n// ─── TelemetryEvent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single structured telemetry record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\n    /// UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Discriminant string, e.g. `\"state_transition\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// The worker that emitted this event.\n    pub worker_id: WorkerId,\n    /// Session identifier (8 hex chars, unique per worker boot).\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// Monotonically increasing counter within the session.\n    pub sequence: u64,\n    /// Bead context when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n    /// Workspace directory when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    /// Event-specific payload as a JSON value.\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Optional duration in milliseconds for timed operations.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// W3C trace-id hex (32 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub trace_id: Option<String>,\n    /// W3C span-id hex (16 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub span_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ─── EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Typed event variants emitted by all NEEDLE components.\n///\n/// Every variant maps to a `TelemetryEvent` with `event_type` matching\n/// the snake_case discriminant name.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum EventKind {\n    // ── Worker lifecycle ──\n    WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStarted {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStopped {\n        reason: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerErrored {\n        error_type: String,\n        error_message: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerExhausted {\n        cycle_count: u64,\n        last_strand: String,\n        /// How many times the waterfall restarted before exhausting.\n        waterfall_restarts: u32,\n        /// Name of each strand that returned WorkCreated (one entry per restart).\n        restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n        /// All strand evaluations in order, across all waterfall passes.\n        /// Each entry is (strand_name, result, duration_ms) for diagnostic visibility.\n        strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n    },\n    WorkerIdle {\n        backoff_seconds: u64,\n    },\n    StateTransition {\n        from: WorkerState,\n        to: WorkerState,\n    },\n    InitStepStarted {\n        step: String,\n    },\n    InitStepCompleted {\n        step: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerBootTimeout {\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Strand evaluation ──\n    StrandEvaluated {\n        strand_name: String,\n        result: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    StrandSkipped {\n        strand_name: String,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    QueueEmpty,\n\n    // ── Bead claim ──\n    ClaimAttempt {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        attempt: u32,\n    },\n    ClaimSuccess {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLost {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLostSkipped {\n        consecutive_losses: u32,\n        threshold: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Bead lifecycle ──\n    BeadReleased {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    BeadReleaseFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    BeadCompleted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    BeadOrphaned {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n\n    // ── Agent dispatch ──\n    DispatchStarted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        agent: String,\n        prompt_len: usize,\n        /// Template name used to build the prompt (e.g., `\"pluck\"`).\n        template_name: String,\n        /// Version tag identifying which variant was used (e.g., `\"pluck-default\"`, `\"pluck-v2\"`).\n        template_version: String,\n        /// SHA-256 hex digest of the rendered prompt content.\n        prompt_hash: String,\n    },\n    DispatchCompleted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        exit_code: i32,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    BuildTimeout {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        timeout_secs: u64,\n    },\n    BuildHeartbeat {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Outcome ──\n    OutcomeClassified {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        exit_code: i32,\n    },\n    OutcomeHandled {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        action: String,\n    },\n    WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        operation: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Health ──\n    HeartbeatEmitted {\n        bead_id: String,\n    },\n    HealthCheck {\n        db_healthy: bool,\n        disk_free_mb: u64,\n        peer_count: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Mend strand ──\n    MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n        lock_path: String,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendDependencyCleaned {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        blocker_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    MendDbRepaired {\n        warnings: u32,\n        fixed: u32,\n    },\n    MendDbRebuilt,\n    MendCycleSummary {\n        beads_released: u32,\n        locks_removed: u32,\n        deps_cleaned: u32,\n        db_repaired: bool,\n        db_rebuilt: bool,\n        agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n        traces_pruned: u32,\n        traces_deleted: u32,\n        workers_deregistered: u32,\n        idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n        rate_limits_cleaned: MendIdleWorkerFlagged {\n        worker_id: String,\n        pid: u32,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendWorkerDeregistered {\n        worker_id: String,\n        pid: u32,\n    },\n Mend strand: maintenance and self-healing.\n//!\n//! Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n//! corruption. If any cleanup is performed, returns `WorkCreated` so the\n//! waterfall restarts from Pluck (released beads may now be claimable).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::{LimitsConfig, MendConfig};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\nuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\nuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n/// cleaned up).\n///\n/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\npub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &Registry,\n    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n    qualified_id: &str,\n) -> Result<u32> {\n    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n    let workers = registry.list()?;\n\n    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut released = 0u32;\n\n    for bead in &all_beads {\n        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n            _ => continue,\n        };\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n        if assignee == qualified_id {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n        tracing::info!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            assignee = %assignee,\n            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n        );\n\n        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: format!(\"orphaned: assignee {} has no live worker\", assignee),\n                });\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n                });\n                released += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n                );\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.to_string(),\n                    assignee: assignee.clone(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(released)\n}\n\n/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct MendSummary {\n    beads_released: u32,\n    locks_removed: u32,\n    deps_cleaned: u32,\n    db_repaired: bool,\n    db_rebuilt: bool,\n    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n    traces_pruned: u32,\n    traces_cleaned: u32,\n    learnings_pruned: u32,\n    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n    orphaned_heartbeats_removed: u32,\n    workers_deregistered: u32,\n    idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n    rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_removed: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_reset: u32,\n}\n\nimpl MendSummary {\n    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n    ///\n    /// Only operations that add or release claimable beads should return true.\n    /// A strand MUST return `WorkCreated` only when it inserts a claimable bead\n    /// into the store that the waterfall will re-scan. No-op cleanup (pruning\n    /// traces, removing stale locks, repairing DB) should return `NoWork`.\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `WorkCreated`:\n    /// - `beads_released > 0`: Orphaned beads were released back to Open status.\n    /// - `deps_cleaned > 0`: Stale dependency links were removed (beads become claimable).\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `NoWork` (maintenance, not work creation):\n    /// - `locks_removed > 0`: Lock file cleanup (doesn't add beads to queue).\n    /// - `db_repaired`: Doctor repair fixed index corruption (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `db_rebuilt`: Full rebuild from JSONL (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `traces_pruned`, `agent_logs_cleaned`, `learnings_*`: File cleanup.\n    ///\n    /// A `WorkCreated` return must be paired with a telemetry event identifying\n    /// the created bead(s) so operators can see what the restart is chasing.\n    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        // Bead release and dependency removal add claimable items to the queue.\n        // Lock removal, DB repair, and DB rebuild are maintenance operations\n        // that don't create new work and must not trigger a waterfall restart.\n        self.beads_released > 0 || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n    }\n}\n\n/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\npub struct MendStrand {\n    config: MendConfig,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity ({adapter}-{worker_id}).\n    /// Used for heartbeat file lookups and registry comparisons to prevent\n    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n    qualified_id: String,\n    registry: Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    log_dir: PathBuf,\n    retention_days: u32,\n    traces_dir: PathBuf,\n    trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n    trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    max_learnings: usize,\n    /// Base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory).\n    state_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup.\n    limits_config: LimitsConfig,\n}","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":3241}}}

function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n418\t    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n419\t    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n420\t    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n421\t    /// stale metadata file.\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n424\t    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n425\t    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n426\t        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n427\t\n428\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n429\t            Ok(e) => e,\n430\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n431\t            Err(e) => {\n432\t                tracing::warn!(\n433\t                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n434\t                    error = %e,\n435\t                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n436\t                );\n437\t                return Ok(());\n438\t            }\n439\t        };\n440\t\n441\t        for entry in entries {\n442\t            let entry = match entry {\n443\t                Ok(e) => e,\n444\t                Err(_) => continue,\n445\t            };\n446\t\n447\t            let path = entry.path();\n448\t            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n449\t                Some(n) => n,\n450\t                None => continue,\n451\t            };\n452\t\n453\t            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n454\t            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n455\t                continue;\n456\t            }\n457\t\n458\t            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n459\t            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n460\t                Some(age) => age,\n461\t                None => continue,\n462\t            };\n463\t\n464\t            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). Orphaned heartbeat file removal ────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Remove heartbeat files that have no matching entry in the worker registry.\n    ///\n    /// This can happen due to:\n    /// - Manual deletion of registry entries\n    /// - Registry corruption\n    /// - Worker crash between registry write and heartbeat file creation\n    ///\n    /// Only removes heartbeat files older than heartbeat_ttl to avoid deleting\n    /// recently orphaned files that might still be in use.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_heartbeats(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        // Read all heartbeat files.\n        let heartbeats = match HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir) {\n            Ok(h) => h,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read heartbeat directory for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Get all registered worker IDs.\n        let registered_ids = match self.registry.list() {\n            Ok(workers) => {\n                let mut ids = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n                for w in workers {\n                    ids.insert(w.id);\n                }\n                ids\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read worker registry for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for hb in heartbeats {\n            // Use qualified_id for comparison; fall back to worker_id for old heartbeats.\n            let worker_key = if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n                &hb.worker_id\n            } else {\n                &hb.qualified_id\n            };\n\n            // Skip if this heartbeat has a matching registry entry.\n            if registered_ids.contains(worker_key) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip our own heartbeat (shouldn't happen, but be safe).\n            if worker_key == &self.qualified_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if the heartbeat is stale (older than TTL).\n            // Only remove stale heartbeats to avoid deleting recently orphaned files.\n            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Heartbeat is orphaned and stale — remove it.\n            let heartbeat_path = hb\n                .heartbeat_file\n                .clone()\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", worker_key)));\n\n            let age_secs = match file_age(&heartbeat_path) {\n                Some(age) => age.as_secs(),\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            match std::fs::remove_file(&heartbeat_path) {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        worker_id = %worker_key,\n                        path = %heartbeat_path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self\n                        .telemetry\n                        .emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedHeartbeatRemoved {\n                            worker_id: worker_key.clone(),\n                            age_secs,\n                        });\n\n                    summary.orphaned_heartbeats_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        path = %heartbeat_path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to remove orphaned heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Remove claim lock files that are not actively held by any process.\n    ///\n    /// This function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n    /// stale metadata file.\n    ///\n    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n                Some(age) => age,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n            // no one is holding it — safe to delete immediately.\n            //\n            // The kernel releases flocks when the holder process dies, so this\n            // probe succeeds immediately for dead PIDs regardless of file age.\n            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n                        );\n                        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed {\n                            lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                            error: e.to_string(),\n                        });\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                        age_secs,\n                    });\n\n                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {","numLines":300,"startLine":200,"totalLines":3241}}}

Worker state registry — shared JSON file tracking all active workers.\n//!\n//! The registry is informational: \"who is running, what are they doing, how\n//! many beads processed.\" It is NOT used for coordination — heartbeats handle\n//! that.\n//!\n//! File: `~/.needle/state/workers.json`\n//! Access: flock-protected read-modify-write (atomic updates).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n///\n/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\npub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    {\n        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n        unsafe {\n            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n            if ret == 0 {\n                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n                return true;\n            }\n            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n            match errno {\n                libc::ESRCH => {\n                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n                    false\n                }\n                libc::EPERM => {\n                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n                    true\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    {\n        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// WorkerEntry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A This helps identify workers that may have failed\n    /// to start, are stuck in dispatch, or have agent adapter problems.\n    ///\n    /// This is a warning/telemetry-only operation — idle workers are NOT\n    /// deregistered (they may be genuinely waiting for work).\n    fn flag_idle_workers(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let workers = self.registry.list()?;\n\n        for worker in workers {\n            // Skip workers that have processed at least one bead.\n            if worker.beads_processed > 0 {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Calculate worker age from started_at.\n            let age = match Utc::now().signed_duration_since(worker.started_at).to_std() {\n                Ok(d) => d,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        worker_id = %worker.id,\n                        \"worker started_at is in the future, skipping idle check\"\n                    );\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let idle_timeout = Duration::from_secs(self.config.idle_timeout);\n\n            if age > idle_timeout {\n                let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    worker_id = %worker.id,\n                    pid = worker.pid,\n                    age_secs,\n                    idle_timeout_secs = self.config.idle_timeout,\n                    \"flagging idle worker (0 beads processed for longer than idle_timeout)\"\n                );\n\n                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendIdleWorkerFlagged {\n                    worker_id: worker.id.clone(),\n                    pid: worker.pid,\n                    age_secs,\n                });\n\n                summary.idle_workers_flagged += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4.9: Rate limiter state cleanup ─────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Clean up rate limiter state files.\n    ///\n    /// - Remove token bucket files for providers no longer in config\n    /// - Reset token buckets with stale last_refill (> 1 hour old) to full capacity\n    fn cleanup_rate_limit_state(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        use crate::rate_limit::TokenBucket;\n\n        let rate_limits_dir = self.state_dir.join(\"rate_limits\");\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&rate_limits_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    dir = %rate_limits_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read rate_limits directory for cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Only consider JSON files (token bucket files).\n            if !name.ends_with(\".json\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Extract provider name from filename (e.g., \"anthropic.json\" -> \"anthropic\").\n            let provider = match name.strip_suffix(\".json\") {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Check if provider exists in config.\n            let provider_in_config = self.limits_config.providers.contains_key(provider);\n\n            if !provider_in_config {\n                // Provider no longer in config — remove the file.\n                let age_secs = match file_age(&path) {\n                    Some(age) => age.as_secs(),\n                    None => continue,\n                };\n\n                match std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                    Ok(()) => {\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            provider,\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            age_secs,\n                            \"removed rate limit state file for provider not in config\"\n                        );\n\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(EventKind::MendRateLimitCleaned {\n                                provider: provider.to_string(),\n                                age_secs,\n                            });\n\n                        summary.rate_limit_providers_removed += 1;\n                        summary.rate_limits_cleaned += 1;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            provider,\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove rate limit state file\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }","numLines":200,"startLine":800,"totalLines":3241}}}

self.actions.lock().unwrap().clone()\n    }\n\n    fn record(&self, action: &str) {\n        self.actions.lock().unwrap().push(action.to_string());\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl BeadStore for IntegrationMockStore {\n    async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n        self.record(\"ready\");\n        // Only return open, unassigned beads (matching real br behavior).\n        Ok(self\n            .beads\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_none())\n            .cloned()\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n        self.record(\"list_all\");\n        Ok(self.beads.lock().unwrap().clone())\n    }\n\n    async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"show:{id}\"));\n        let beads = self.beads.lock().unwrap();\n        let bead = beads\n            .iter()\n            .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n            .cloned()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| make_bead_with_id(id.as_ref()));\n        Ok(bead)\n    }\n\n    async fn claim(&self, id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"claim:{id}:{actor}\"));\n        let mut beads = self.beads.lock().unwrap();\n        if let Some(bead) = beads.iter_mut().find(|b| b.id == *id) {\n            bead.status = BeadStatus::InProgress;\n            bead.assignee = Some(actor.to_string());\n            Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead.clone()))\n        } else {\n            Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n                reason: \"not found\".to_string(),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn release(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"release:{id}\"));\n        // Remove released beads from the list to prevent infinite re-selection loops.\n        // In real usage, released beads get labels (deferred, failure-count) that\n        // filter them out, but the mock doesn't simulate full label-based filtering.\n        let mut beads = self.beads.lock().unwrap();\n        beads.retain(|b| b.id != *id);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn reopen(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"reopen:{id}\"));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"labels:{id}\"));\n        Ok(vec![])\n    }\n\n    async fn add_label(&self, id: &BeadId, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"add_label:{id}:{label}\"));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn remove_label(&self, id: &BeadId, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"remove_label:{id}:{label}\"));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn create_bead(&self, title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"create_bead:{title}\"));\n        Ok(BeadId::from(\"alert-new\"))\n    }\n\n    async fn add_dependency(&self, blocker_id: &BeadId, blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n        self.record(&format!(\"add_dep:{}:{}\", blocker_id, blocked_id));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n        Ok(RepairReport::default())\n    }\n    async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n        Ok(RepairReport::default())\n    }\n    async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_bead_with_id(id: &str) -> Bead {\n    Bead {\n        id: BeadId::from(id),\n        title: format!(\"Test bead {id}\"),\n        body: Some(\"Implement something useful\".to_string()),\n        priority: 1,\n        status: BeadStatus::Open,\n        assignee: None,\n        labels: vec![],\n        workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n        dependencies: vec![],\n        dependents: vec![],\n        created_at: Utc::now(),\n        updated_at: Utc::now(),\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_bead(id: &str, priority: u8) -> Bead {\n    let mut bead = make_bead_with_id(id);\n    bead.priority = priority;\n    bead\n}\n\nfn test_adapter(name: &str, template: &str, timeout_secs: u64) -> AgentAdapter {\n    AgentAdapter {\n        name: name.to_string(),\n        description: None,\n        agent_cli: \"bash\".to_string(),\n        version_command: None,\n        input_method: InputMethod::Stdin,\n        invoke_template: template.to_string(),\n        environment: HashMap::new(),\n        timeout_secs,\n        provider: None,\n        model: None,\n        token_extraction: needle::dispatch::TokenExtraction::None,\n        output_transform: None,\n    }\n}\n\nfn test_config(adapter_name: &str, workspace_home: &std::path::Path) -> Config {\n    let mut config = Config::default();\n    config.worker.idle_action = IdleAction::Exit;\n    config.agent.default = adapter_name.to_string();\n    config.agent.timeout = 10;\n    config.self_modification.hot_reload = false;\n    // Match the test bead workspace so the remote-store-switch logic\n    // doesn't fire (it would try to create a BrCliBeadStore).\n    config.workspace.default = std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\");\n    // Isolate workspace home so the registry doesn't leak between tests.\n    config.workspace.home = workspace_home.to_path_buf();\n    config\n}\n\n/// Returns `(Worker, TempDir)` — the TempDir must be kept alive for the test duration.\nfn make_worker_with_adapter(\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    adapter_name: &str,\n    template: &str,\n    timeout_secs: u64,\n) -> (Worker, tempfile::TempDir) {\n    let home_dir = tempfile::tempdir().expect(\"failed to create temp dir for test workspace home\");\n    let config = test_config(adapter_name, home_dir.path());\n    let mut worker = Worker::new(config, \"test-worker\".to_string(), store);\n\n    let adapter = test_adapter(adapter_name, template, timeout_secs);\n    let mut adapters = HashMap::new();\n    adapters.insert(adapter_name.to_string(), adapter);\n    worker.set_dispatcher(Dispatcher::with_adapters(\n        adapters,\n        Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n        10,\n    ));\n\n    (worker, home_dir)\n}\n\n// ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n// Test 1: End-to-end single worker\n// ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn end_to_end_single_bead_success() {\n    // A single bead exists, = worker.run().await.unwrap();\n\n    // Worker should process the bead and then exhaust (no more beads).\n    assert!(\n        result == WorkerState::Stopped || result == WorkerState::Exhausted,\n        \"expected terminal state, got {:?}\",\n        result\n    );\n    assert!(\n        worker.beads_processed() >= 1,\n        \"expected at least 1 bead processed, got {}\",\n        worker.beads_processed()\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn end_to_end_worker_loops_to_next_bead() {\n    // Two beads exist; worker processes both.\n    let beads = config.workspace.home = _home_dir.path().to_path_buf();\n\n    let mut worker = Worker::new(config, \"test-worker\".to_string(), store);\n\n    let adapter = test_adapter(\"echo-test\", \"echo done\",
